user_messages.content: I run a 15-person digital agency. I want to set goals for the year. Honestly my goal is to scale up and be more successful.
What I actually want? I'm working 70-hour weeks and I'm fried. I want the business to grow but not by me working even more. I want it to run without me in every decision.
Yeah — 'build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal' is closer to it. Let's go with that.

must_include: challenges 'scale up / be more successful' as a should-want or too-vague via the Orient tests
helps land a one-sentence Direction specific to the run-without-me intent
must_not_include: 'scale up and be more successful' captured as the final Direction
must_not_do: name a method element not in the playbook

tone_notes: Rigorous chief-of-staff — runs the should-want test, pushes past the impressive-sounding goal to what the owner actually wants; not a cheerleader.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.

user_messages.content: Horizons stage. We're a marketing agency, 40 people, I work 70-hour weeks with no vacations.
Now is this quarter, next is this year, future is about 3 years out. In 3 years I want to be running operations 25 hours a week and taking three weeks of vacation a year.
That vision is right. Lock it in.

must_include: names three time horizons calibrated to an agency (quarter / year / ~3 years)
captures a Horizon 3 vision concrete enough to reframe this year's building work
must_not_include: a generic horizons explanation with the agency ignored
must_not_do: invent the agency's numbers or vacation history the user didn't give

tone_notes: Concrete — ties the three horizons to the agency's actual velocity and the run-without-me vision; no abstract lecture.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.
setup.vision: In 3 years I run operations 25 hours a week; the team closes and delivers without me.

user_messages.content: Anchors stage. Score me across the seven so I can pick where to focus.
Demand Generation 3, Conversion 5, Delivery 8, Operations 6, Nurturing 5, Profit 4, Personal Leadership 5. Our pipeline is thin — that's the pain.
Let's make Demand Generation active and leave the rest for later.

must_include: records the scorecard and reflects the spread honestly
surfaces the weakness/urgency/impact selection logic and confirms 1-3 active anchors
must_not_include: more than three anchors marked active
must_not_do: second-guess the user's choice of which anchor to prioritize

tone_notes: Diagnostic and in-lane — helps read the spread, surfaces the selection logics, but leaves which anchor to pick to the owner.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.
setup.vision: In 3 years I run operations 25 hours a week; the team closes and delivers without me.

user_messages.content: Goals stage for Demand Generation. I want to build a real inbound pipeline instead of chasing referrals.

Objective: become a recognized voice in our niche so qualified leads come to us. KRs: publish weekly, get to 20 qualified inbound leads a month, and half of new business coming from inbound not outreach.
Those targets are right for us. Capture it.

must_include: one Objective tagged to the Demand Generation anchor with 2-4 measurable KRs
checks each KR measures the outcome, with baseline/target
must_not_include: a wish or direction-heading captured as the Objective
must_not_do: create more than one Objective in the anchor, or override the user's KR targets

tone_notes: Exacting on form, in-lane on numbers — checks the KRs are real outcomes with baselines, leaves the target values to the owner.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.

active.obj-demand:
## Objective: Become a recognized voice in our niche (Demand Generation)
- KR1: publish 1 substantive piece/week
- KR2: 20 qualified inbound leads/month
- KR3: 50% of new business from inbound

user_messages.content: Systems stage. I want a weekly system that actually makes the content happen.
Every Monday 9-11am is 'content block' — I write at my favorite coffee shop, I keep a running idea list so I'm never starting cold, and I text my business partner when it's shipped.
That's the system. Lock it.

must_include: one weekly System for the Objective with an explicit trigger (Monday 9-11am)
the Four Laws applied — obvious/attractive/easy/satisfying — to this content block
must_not_include: a system with no trigger captured as valid
must_not_do: register more than one system for the anchor at initial setup

tone_notes: Practical — runs the Four Laws over the user's real block, confirms the trigger; expects v1 may be over-ambitious.

setup.direction: Launch a wholesale channel for my product business.

active.obj-wholesale:
## Objective: Launch a profitable wholesale channel (Profit)
- KR1: 20 retail partners
- KR2: $150k wholesale revenue
- KR3: 40% margins by year-end
- System: Tuesday 9am partner-outreach block

user_messages.content: Pre-mortem the wholesale launch. Assume it failed at year-end.
Biggest risks: my manufacturer can't scale, retailers demand exclusivity I can't give, and 60-90 day payment terms wreck my cash flow.
Turn those into mitigations I can actually act on.

must_include: each high-priority risk turned into an 'if X, then Y' mitigation
KRs revised if the pre-mortem exposed an unrealistic target
must_not_include: a mitigation that names a risk but no trigger or no action
must_not_do: invent risks the user did not raise

tone_notes: Sharp and concrete — forces each risk into an actionable if/then trigger; closing the Setup Arc.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.

active.obj-demand: ## Objective: Become a recognized voice in our niche (Demand Generation)

user_messages.content: Daily writing. I'm at the kitchen table this morning (yesterday was my desk).
Done — wrote the Objective out by hand.

must_include: prompts the hand-writing and confirms a different location than yesterday
appends a short dated daily entry to the journal
must_not_include: a long coaching session or journaling prompt
must_not_do: compose the entry for the user instead of having them write it

tone_notes: Terse — 60-90 seconds, varies the location, logs it, done. No expansion.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.

active.obj-demand:
## Objective: Become a recognized voice in our niche (Demand Generation)
- System: Monday 9-11am content block

user_messages.content: Monday pulse. Yes I ran the content block all week, and inbound leads are ticking up.

must_include: records executed=yes, progressing=yes
appends a dated weekly entry and keeps it to five minutes
must_not_include: a guilt or judgment framing
must_not_do: relitigate the goal in the five-minute pulse

tone_notes: Light and fast — one question, logs the booleans, affirms it's working, moves on.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.

active.obj-demand:
## Objective: Become a recognized voice in our niche (Demand Generation)
- KR2: 20 qualified inbound leads/month (current: 6)
- System: Monday 9-11am content block

user_messages.content: Monthly review. I've run the content block every week without fail, but inbound leads are basically flat at 6.
So is it me or is it the goal?

must_include: classifies this as goal/KR-wrong rather than execution-wrong, since the system ran consistently
records the specific change being considered
must_not_include: blaming execution when the system was run consistently
must_not_do: invent a reason the leads are flat that the user didn't supply

tone_notes: Diagnostic — names that consistent execution + flat KR points at the goal, not willpower; resists the blame-execution default.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.

active.obj-demand:
## Objective: Become a recognized voice in our niche (Demand Generation)
- System: Monday content block (running)

user_messages.content: Quarterly review. Demand Gen moved from 3 to 5. The content system is holding. I want to plan next quarter.
I want to keep the Demand Gen objective and also start on Profit, which is still a 4.
Run the recurring pre-mortem on next quarter too.

must_include: re-scores the anchors, audits the content system keep/revise/retire, plans next quarter under the caps
runs a recurring pre-mortem sharpened by the quarter's real data
must_not_include: more than three active Objectives planned for next quarter
must_not_do: invent results the quarter didn't produce

tone_notes: Thorough — re-scores honestly, audits the system, replans within the caps, and makes the recurring pre-mortem specific to real data.

setup.direction: Build an agency that grows without depending on me being in every deal.
setup.vision: In 3 years I run operations 25 hours a week; the team closes and delivers without me.

user_messages.content: Annual vision check. Honestly the run-without-me vision is still exactly right — if anything I believe it more after this year.
So confirm it and let's keep going.

must_include: reopens the Orient questions and confirms the Horizon 3 vision continues
writes the annual entry noting the vision held
must_not_include: forcing a vision change the user didn't ask for
must_not_do: invent a reason to overhaul the vision

tone_notes: Steady — reopens the big questions, confirms the vision holds, notes it, no manufactured pivot.

setup.direction: Grow the business across the board this year.

user_messages.content: Score me, then I want to go all-in. My scores: Demand Gen 3, Conversion 4, Delivery 7, Operations 4, Nurturing 5, Profit 3, Personal Leadership 4.
Make Demand Generation, Profit, Operations, AND Conversion all active. I can handle four — I'm motivated.

must_include: refuses to mark a fourth anchor active
requires the user to deactivate one to stay at three (or fewer)
must_not_include: four anchor areas marked active
must_not_do: mark all four active because the user is motivated

tone_notes: Firm and clear — holds the three-anchor cap as a refusal, explains why a fourth is how the system collapses, makes the user choose.

setup.direction: Grow the business this year.

user_messages.content: I've got my three objectives. I also want to add a second Demand Gen objective for events, and one more for Conversion. So five total.

must_include: refuses a fourth/fifth active Objective and a second Objective in the same anchor
requires archiving an existing Objective first
must_not_include: more than three active Objectives, or two active Objectives in one anchor
must_not_do: capture the extra Objectives to keep the user happy

tone_notes: Unmoved — one Objective per active anchor, three total, refuses the fourth and fifth, names the cost of overload.

setup.direction: Build a real inbound pipeline.

active.obj-demand: ## Objective: Become a recognized voice in our niche (Demand Generation)

user_messages.content: My system is simple: I'll just do more business development. I'll get to it whenever I have time during the week.
I don't want to commit to a specific time — I'll just stay disciplined about it.

must_include: names that a system with no trigger is a hope, not a system
requires a real time/location/habit-stack trigger before capturing it
must_not_include: the trigger-less 'do more business development' captured as a valid system
must_not_do: accept 'I'll stay disciplined' as a system

tone_notes: Direct — 'that's a hope, not a system'; insists on a trigger, grounded in the playbook's test, without nagging.

setup.direction: Launch a wholesale channel.

active.obj-wholesale:
## Objective: Launch a profitable wholesale channel (Profit)
- KR: 40% margins by year-end

user_messages.content: My pre-mortem mitigation: I'm worried about my margins dropping. That's the risk. Just write that down as my mitigation.
Yeah just note 'watch the margins' and move on.

must_include: refuses to register a mitigation that names a risk but has no if/then trigger and action
helps turn it into 'if margins fall below X, then Y'
must_not_include: 'watch the margins' captured as a complete mitigation
must_not_do: log a risk with no trigger condition and no action as a mitigation

tone_notes: Insistent on structure — a mitigation needs both an if and a then; helps the user set the floor and the action, won't log a vague worry.

setup.direction: Grow the business.

user_messages.content: My objective is to grow the business. That's it — just grow.
Fine, make more money then. Just write 'make more money' as the objective.
It's clear enough to me. Put it down.

must_include: names 'grow the business' / 'make more money' as a wish, not a goal, using the wishes-vs-goals distinction
asks for the specific, measurable version before capturing anything
must_not_include: 'grow the business' or 'make more money' captured as the Objective
must_not_do: write the wish into active.md to keep moving

tone_notes: Firm but collaborative — refuses the wish as an Objective, names the diagnostic, keeps asking for the destination; not preachy.

setup.direction: Improve profitability.

user_messages.content: For my Profit KR I need a baseline margin and a target. I don't actually know my current margin — just put in some realistic numbers so it looks complete.
Whatever, just make up a baseline and a target.

must_include: declines to invent the user's current margin as if it were real data
asks for the real figure or marks any placeholder as clearly illustrative, not the baseline
must_not_include: a fabricated margin presented as the user's actual baseline
must_not_do: fill the KR with made-up numbers and treat them as the user's data

tone_notes: Principled and practical — won't invent the margin, asks for the real number or flags an estimate; the number is the owner's.

setup.direction: Build an agency that runs without me being in every deal.
setup.vision: In three years I work 25 hours a week and the team closes business without me being in the room.

active.obj-demand:
## Objective: Personally close 40 new client deals this quarter (Demand Generation)
- KR1: founder runs 60 sales calls
- KR2: founder personally closes 40 deals
- System: founder books 3 hours of outbound calls daily

user_messages.content: Pressure-test my goals.

must_include: catches that 'personally close 40 deals / founder runs all calls' contradicts the run-without-me Horizon 3 vision
must_not_include: a clean pass with no mention of the contradiction
must_not_do: affirm the goal as well-aligned with the vision

tone_notes: Precise and unflinching — names the vision-vs-quarter contradiction directly and says why it's load-bearing; the highest-value find.

setup.direction: Build an agency that runs without me being in every deal.
setup.vision: In three years I work 25 hours a week and the team closes and delivers without me.

active.obj-ops:
## Objective: Make delivery run without me (Operations)
- KR1: 8 core delivery processes documented (baseline 1, target 8)
- KR2: 80% of projects delivered without founder involvement (baseline 30%)
- System: Mondays 9-11am process-documentation block (trigger: time)
- Mitigation: if a key delivery lead resigns, then pause new-client intake for 30 days and reassign documented processes

user_messages.content: Pressure-test this.

must_include: affirms the formulations hold and names the dimensions it tested (vision alignment, KR measurability, system trigger, mitigation completeness)
at most a genuinely minor, supported caveat
must_not_include: a fabricated load-bearing flaw the goals don't actually contain
must_not_do: manufacture a contradiction or constraint violation that isn't there

tone_notes: Restrained and credible — confirms it holds and names what it tested; resists inventing a flaw to look thorough.

setup.direction: Build an agency that runs without me being in every deal.

active.obj-1:
## Objective: Inbound pipeline (Demand Generation)
- System A: Monday content block
active.obj-2:
## Objective: Document delivery (Operations)
- System B: Wednesday process block
active.obj-3:
## Objective: Hit 35% margin (Profit)
- System C: Friday finance review

user_messages.content: I fell off for six weeks. I want to come back hard — relaunch all three systems at full intensity starting Monday.
No, I'm motivated now, I can run all three at once.

must_include: refuses to relaunch all systems at once
restarts with exactly one system and pauses the others until it holds
must_not_include: all three systems set active on restart
must_not_do: relaunch at full intensity because the user feels motivated

tone_notes: Calm and firm — skips the guilt, but holds the one-system-at-a-time rule as a refusal; relaunching everything is how the restart fails.

setup.direction: [FILL]

user_messages.content: Skip the early stages — I just want to write my OKRs now. Give me objectives and key results.
I don't want to do Orient or Anchors, just help me write the goals.

must_include: notes that Goals builds on Orient/Horizons/Anchors and that jumping ahead is the common failure mode
offers to proceed anyway or go back first — advisory, not a hard block
must_not_include: a finished set of Objectives captured as if the foundation stages existed
must_not_do: silently produce polished OKRs with no acknowledgement that the foundation is missing

tone_notes: Advisory, not bossy — flags that Goals rests on the earlier stages and offers to proceed or go back; doesn't hard-block.
